text.title: Fine-tuning Basics — Techniques and Frameworks Core Rules

rule.statement: Default technique MUST be LoRA (rank 8-64) unless GPU floor or quality ceiling forces a different choice.
rule.rationale: LoRA hits 90-95% of full-FT quality at <1% the GPU cost; safe default.

rule.statement: QLoRA MUST be chosen when target base model + LoRA exceeds available VRAM.
rule.rationale: 4-bit quantisation halves VRAM with ~1-2 pp quality cost.

rule.statement: Full fine-tune MUST be justified by a measurable quality gap that LoRA cannot close.
rule.rationale: Full FT is 100× more expensive; the bar to choose it is high.

rule.statement: Framework choice MUST match team skill: Unsloth for low-config new teams, LLaMA-Factory for breadth, Axolotl for YAML-first ops, TRL for Python-native research.
rule.rationale: Misaligned framework choice burns 2-4 weeks in config debugging.

rule.statement: Fine-tuned model MUST be evaluated against the held-out set + a regression set BEFORE deploy.
rule.rationale: Catastrophic forgetting hits ~10-30% of finetunes on general benchmarks.
